mnemonics: CREATE: Components of Relations Together Easily
CARROT: Cartesian Relations Are Really Straightforward To Understand
keyPoints: Definition: The Cartesian product of two sets A and B is the set of all ordered pairs (a, b) where a is in A and b is in B.
Notation: A × B = {(a, b) | a ∈ A, b ∈ B}
Example: The Cartesian product of {1, 2} and {a, b} is {(1, a), (1, b), (2, a), (2, b)}
Properties: The Cartesian product is commutative, associative, and has an identity element (the empty set)
Types: Discrete, finite, and infinite Cartesian products
